methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.1B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $2.5B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Skyryse, a Series C-stage space and aerospace company with a current post-money valuation of $1.15B and $500M+ raised to date, is evaluated against a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, and Isar Aerospace.

Two valuation methods triangulate to support the range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x multiplier to the $1.1B last post-money valuation, implying $2.0B. Peer-median anchoring across 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median of $1.6B, with a $1.0B–$2.5B range (Isar Aerospace at the low end, Axiom Space at $2.5B). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 53.2x across the sector) cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, typical for private firms. The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median of $1.8B. The current $1.15B valuation sits below the triangulated low bound, suggesting potential upside. Key uncertainty centers on revenue trajectory and path to cash-flow generation relative to comparables, which the available data does not address.
